title: Size of the largest BST in a Binary Tree

content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Size of the largest BST in a Binary Tree". Refer to LeetCode description: https://leetcode.com/problems/maximum-sum-bst-in-binary-tree/
constraints: 1 <= Number of nodes in the tree <= 10^4
content.inputFormat: The input is the root of a binary tree where each node has a unique value.
content.outputFormat: The function should return the maximum sum of a Binary Search Tree (BST) in the binary tree.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a depth-first search (DFS) approach to traverse the binary tree and check each subtree to see if it is a BST. If it is a BST, we calculate its sum and update the maximum sum if necessary.

code.Python3:
class Solution:
            def maxSumBST(self, root: TreeNode) -> int:
               self.max_sum = 0
               self.dfs(root)
               return self.max_sum

            def dfs(self, node):
               if not node:
                  return [0, float('inf'), float('-inf'), 1]

               left = self.dfs(node.left)
               right = self.dfs(node.right)

               sum_val = left[0] + right[0] + node.val
               min_val = min(left[1], right[1], node.val)
               max_val = max(left[2], right[2], node.val)
               is_bst = left[3] == 1 and right[3] == 1 and node.val > left[2] and node.val < right[1]

               if is_bst:
                  self.max_sum = max(self.max_sum, sum_val)

               return [sum_val, min_val, max_val, is_bst]
